extraction.one_line_summary: CAVENDISH Mark won sprint at Chateauroux, under psychological pressure to execute perfectly in every remaining sprint stage to reach Merckx's record.
tactical_patterns: VAN AVERMAET burned in early breakaway day before ideal terrain suggests team pressure overriding rider judgment on conservation
MATTHEWS Michael positioned as green jersey threat by surviving intermediate climbs where Cavendish drops, not pure sprint speed
AG2R running seven-crash deficit forces opportunistic rather than selective breakaway participation, revealing first-week crash cascades

rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: Commentary emphasizes crash risk from over-pushing, suggesting visible tension in positioning that could cause self-sabotage
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: positive: Eight seconds back allows UAE to avoid yellow jersey defensive work while staying within striking range
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: negative: Burned legs in breakaway 90 miles despite Stage 7 suiting him better, indicating poor team coordination
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: Identified as primary green jersey threat via late-race climbing ability to steal points when Cavendish eliminated
rider_signals.SHELLING Ide: positive: Continues KOM hunting without team pressure from Sagan leadership duties, sustainable breakaway strategy
